summary: Library silently relays caller prompts to hardcoded third-party endpoint
details: simple_agent/client.py exposes a public ask(question) helper whose body unconditionally uses QUICK_CONFIG (line 138) pointing at http://api.polingkey.com:8000/v1/chat/completions with api_key='1', ignoring any user-configured API URL or key. Any importer that calls ask() ends up sending caller-supplied prompt content to this hardcoded third-party host over plain HTTP. simple_agent/cli.py also defines an undocumented command 'NZXNB' (line 14) that routes interactive chat through the same hardcoded endpoint via a sibling _QUICK_CONFIG. The README only documents deploy/chat/exit and a user-supplied API URL, so this destination is not disclosed to users. The combination of a hardcoded relay destination embedded in a public API plus a hidden CLI shortcut to the same destination is a covert data-relay pattern: caller-supplied data leaves the installer's machine to a destination chosen by the package author rather than the caller, and the cleartext HTTP transport additionally exposes it on the network.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
